layman_headline: Market fear is thick and no alt setup is clean enough to pull the trigger.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is in a 'Fear' zone at 28/100, and Bitcoin dominance is high at 56.2%, meaning money is flowing out of smaller coins and into Bitcoin. Every altcoin candidate we screened has significant problems: DEXE doubled in a week and is extremely overbought, ETHFI has strong chart structure but almost zero room before hitting resistance, GRASS just printed a bearish engulfing candle despite a big dip, and the rest lack reliable data or clear setups. In this environment, the best move is patience — waiting for a cleaner entry rather than forcing a trade.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The market is fearful and altcoins are underperforming. A better buying opportunity will likely emerge in the coming days as fear reaches extremes or a clear technical setup develops.
expert_reasoning: I evaluated all 10 candidates systematically. DEXE (chart bias +0.40, RSI 77.18, +100.7% in 7 days) is the strongest mover but is textbook overbought — chasing here contradicts swing discipline. ETHFI (chart bias +0.60, bullish marubozu, RSI 51) has the best chart structure, but resistance at $0.40 is only 2.5% above current price, yielding a 0.11x R:R ratio which is unacceptable. GRASS dropped 22% in 7 days which could be a mean-reversion play, but the bearish engulfing candle and -0.90 chart bias score say sellers are still in control. MON shows a bullish marubozu but sits below all moving averages with a 0.46x R:R. BTC dominance at 56.2% actively suppresses alt performance. Fear & Greed at 28 is improving (was 24.6 avg last week) but not yet at extreme fear levels (sub-20) where contrarian buys have the best historical edge. Preserving capital is the correct call.
risk_factors: High BTC dominance (56.2%) continues to drain alt liquidity — any alt position swims against the current
Stablecoin market cap shrinking by $10B since May signals capital leaving crypto entirely
Geopolitical escalation (US-Iran strikes) could trigger a broader risk-off move
watch_list: Fear & Greed dropping below 20 (extreme fear) — historically signals high-probability contrarian buy zones
ETHFI breaking above $0.40 resistance with volume — would confirm bullish chart structure and open upside
BTC dominance peaking and reversing below 55% — would signal alt season rotation beginning
market_context: Bitcoin is consolidating around $64K with low volatility while Fear & Greed sits at 28 (Fear, but improving from 24.6 weekly average). High BTC dominance at 56.2% and shrinking stablecoin supply create a hostile environment for altcoin swing trades.
context_banner: Market fear is improving but altcoin setups remain too weak to justify capital deployment — disciplined patience is the edge here.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 28 (Fear) is improving from 24.6 weekly average but hasn't reached extreme fear levels where contrarian buys historically perform best.
DEXE RSI at 77 is overbought and dangerous for entry; no other candidate shows oversold RSI conditions that would signal a high-probability bounce.
Stablecoin market cap shrunk $10B since May and US-Iran strikes add macro uncertainty — both bearish for risk assets and alt deployment.
Robinhood L2 sparking ETH optimism is a medium-term positive for ETH ecosystem (ETHFI), but not yet translating into strong alt price action.
No volume breakout detected on any candidate — the most reliable signal in our track record (100% win rate) is absent, reinforcing the HOLD decision.
